vitals.distance: 153 km (Gran Fondo), 112 km (Medio Fondo), 26 km (Time Trial)
vitals.elevation_gain: 1176 m (Gran Fondo), 993 m (Medio Fondo), 225 m (Time Trial)
vitals.terrain: Coastal and Irish backcountry, rolling and undulating with climbs
vitals.participation_trend: First UCI Gran Fondo event in Ireland

course_description.overview: UCI Gran Fondo Ireland is the first-ever Gran Fondo event in Ireland, part of the UCI Gran Fondo World Series. The event features multiple distance options set against the rugged backdrop of Ireland's west coast and County Sligo's picturesque landscapes. The course is designed along the coastline and Irish backcountry, with full road closures in Sligo creating a professional race atmosphere.

key_segments.name: Time Trial Course
key_segments.description: Starts and finishes in Sligo city centre, heading southeast with undulating terrain. Features three climbs: small climb between km 3-4, two climbs between km 12-14 and km 22-23. Highest point is 56m above sea level, making it predominantly flat with slight undulations.

key_segments.name: Gran Fondo Main Course
key_segments.description: Winds through County Sligo's picturesque landscapes along the Wild Atlantic Way with significant climbs. Designed to evoke the feel of a major pro race with professional-level support and atmosphere.

key_segments.name: Medio Fondo Course
key_segments.description: Tailored for seasoned riders over 60 and all female categories, featuring rolling countryside and scenic Emilia-Romagna-style terrain with 993m of elevation gain.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: The Gran Fondo presents moderate difficulty with 1176m of elevation spread across 153 km, resulting in an average gradient of 0.77%. The terrain is characterized by rolling and undulating roads with multiple climbs rather than sustained steep sections. The time trial is relatively flat with only 225m of climbing over 26 km. The coastal setting and Irish backcountry provide scenic but potentially exposed conditions. Overall, this is an accessible gran fondo suitable for intermediate to advanced riders rather than elite-only competition.

logistics.getting_there: Sligo is located approximately 2.5 hours from Dublin by car. The event takes place in northwest Ireland in County Sligo.
logistics.accommodation: Not specified in available sources. Sligo town will have standard accommodation options as a regional hub.
logistics.registration_details: Event is part of UCI Gran Fondo World Series calendar. Participants can choose between Gran Fondo (153 km), Medio Fondo (112 km), or Time Trial (26 km) categories. Registration details not fully specified in sources.
logistics.bike_logistics: Full road closures in Sligo create a controlled racing environment. Professional-level support and atmosphere similar to major pro races.
logistics.local_considerations: Sligo County Council supports the event with full road closures. The region is known for the Wild Atlantic Way coastal scenery and features the backdrop of Benbulben mountain. Weather conditions typical of northwest Ireland should be expected (potentially wet and windy).

final_verdict.one_liner: A landmark inaugural UCI Gran Fondo bringing notable racing infrastructure to Ireland's stunning west coast with accessible distance options and professional-level organization.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Intermediate to advanced cyclists seeking a prestigious UCI-sanctioned event with multiple distance options. Ideal for European riders wanting to qualify for UCI Gran Fondo World Championships. Suitable for riders aged 60+ and female categories via the Medio Fondo option. Best suited for those who value scenic coastal riding and don't require extreme elevation or technical challenges.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, particularly for European-based riders and those seeking to experience Ireland's cycling infrastructure development. The inaugural status, professional organization, and scenic backdrop justify participation. The multiple distance options make it accessible to various skill levels. However, travel costs from outside Europe may impact overall value proposition.
pro_tips: Book accommodation early in Sligo as the inaugural event will attract significant international interest with 3000+ expected riders
Arrive several days early to acclimate to Irish weather conditions and scout the course terrain
Prepare for potentially wet and windy conditions typical of northwest Ireland's Atlantic coast
Consider the Time Trial on June 29 as a qualifier opportunity for UCI Gran Fondo World Championships before committing to the full Gran Fondo distance on June 30
